_meta.description: Pokémon species definitions for Hex Battle Simulator. HP values are inflated for spatial format survivability. Move pools list 8 valid moves per species in priority order: terrain → status → ranged → physical. Players choose 4 from the 8 during the selection phase.

pokemon.name: Pikachu
pokemon.description: The Electric Mouse Pokémon. Fast and versatile with a powerful special attack and reliable terrain control through Electro Web. Low bulk but excellent Speed tier.

pokemon.name: Charizard
pokemon.description: The Flame Pokémon. Highest Sp. Atk and Speed in the roster. Excels at denying tiles with Will-O-Wisp and boosting own Fire moves with Sunny Day. Air Slash bypasses terrain for reliable ranged coverage.

pokemon.name: Gardevoir
pokemon.description: The Embrace Pokémon. Highest Sp. Atk in the roster alongside enormous Sp. Def bulk. Misty Terrain protects allies from stat drops. Calm Mind turns Gardevoir into a late-game sweeper. Average Speed is the main limitation.

pokemon.name: Lucario
pokemon.description: The Aura Pokémon. Exceptional mixed attacker with high ATK and Sp. Atk. Extreme Speed provides reliable priority. Aura Sphere never misses and bypasses fog. Metal Sound Field drains enemy Sp. Def over time for setup synergy.

pokemon.name: Absol
pokemon.description: The Disaster Pokémon. Highest Attack in the roster. A high-risk high-reward Pokémon with fragile defenses. Night Slash and Sucker Punch hit extremely hard. Perish Trap creates powerful late-game pressure. Hail chips away at non-Ice types every turn.

pokemon.name: Gengar
pokemon.description: The Shadow Pokémon. Highest Speed in the roster. The synergy king: Will-O-Wisp + Hex deals devastating doubled damage. Toxic Spikes drains Sp. Def every turn. Destiny Bond punishes aggressive opponents. Lowest HP — needs careful positioning.
